articles.full_text:
Expert Offers Tips for Managing Stress During Extreme Heat
Trish Svoboda/Image courtesy of Canva
Extreme heat can affect more than just physical health. Mental health experts say rising temperatures can increase irritability, frustration, and anxiety. According to Elaine Johannes, a K-State health professional, prolonged exposure to hot weather can take a toll on emotional well-being, especially when people feel overwhelmed by climate-related concerns and dangerous weather conditions.
To help manage stress during periods of extreme heat, experts recommend focusing on factors that can be controlled. That includes recognizing the signs of overheating, seeking shade or air-conditioned spaces, staying hydrated and limiting time outdoors when temperatures are at their highest.
Planning ahead is also important for people who work or exercise outside. Scheduling activities during cooler parts of the day can help reduce heat exposure.
Johannes also encourages people to show patience and compassion toward themselves and others. Simple breathing exercises, such as slow, controlled breathing, can help reduce stress and irritability. Taking regular physical and mental breaks can also improve well-being during prolonged heat events.
